insights.name: chat_to_publish_gap
insights.studentQuestion: Which synthetic users have high Coworker usage but no published documents?
insights.expectedPattern: Churn-risk and a meaningful minority of casual users have high Coworker message counts with zero published documents.

insights.name: power_user_publish_rate
insights.studentQuestion: Do power users publish at a higher rate than casual users?
insights.expectedPattern: Power users own more documents, publish more often, attach more files, and are more likely to create Squad agents.

insights.name: squad_agent_onboarding_cliff
insights.studentQuestion: How many users create or configure Squad agents compared with users who send Coworker messages?
insights.expectedPattern: Squad agent rows are sparse compared with Coworker message usage, matching the course narrative that Squad has high interest but lower activation.

insights.name: draft_review_pressure
insights.studentQuestion: How many active users create multiple private drafts but never publish?
insights.expectedPattern: Several users have multiple non-archived unpublished documents and high chat activity, suggesting a draft-review bridge problem rather than low engagement.

notes: The seeder expands this scenario into deterministic schema-valid rows.
There is no user table in the current Convex schema, so persona is encoded in userId and duplicated in the seeder summary.
The data is synthetic but designed to support realistic Week 3 and Week 4 product-discovery queries.
